meta.setting: Hard-rock mines, tunnel mouths, ore sheds, and pay lines around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with mining labor, company pressure, underground danger, debt, and reputation

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold deposits, mining communities, and work pressure in the region.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, old buildings, road supply, and county context.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Hard-Rock Men cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented work note, lamp warning, or powder-house saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Drill and Powder Crews
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing them as underground miners who understand blasting, claim politics, bad air, and pay trouble
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate them around hard-rock mining labor rather than gold-rush romance.
Tie future moves to drilling rhythm, powder handling, cave-in danger, bad air, pay disputes, ore theft, crew loyalty, and company pressure.
Make the page useful for underground scenes, pay-line arguments, rescue work, claim sabotage, and camp politics.
Keep the language grimy and specific: quartz dust, candle stubs, wet timber, drill steel, powder smoke, coughs, mule sweat, and lunch pails.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let every sentence have quartz dust in the hinge. The mine pays in ounces, coughs, silence, and men who stop singing.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A mining scene should ask who owns the risk, who owns the ore, and which man knows the tunnel is no longer sound.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western mining labor language only.
Keep underground knowledge practical and human.
The generated page must include image path images/hard-rock-men.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not romanticize cave-ins, sickness, or labor exploitation
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about mining labor, underground risk, company pressure, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
